commit.message:
Add personal best tracking with confetti animation

Backend:
- /api/submit now returns is_new_pb (bool) and prev_best, computed by
  combining COUNT+MAX into a single pre-insert query (no schema change)

Frontend:
- PB badge on each unlocked scene card (⭐ PB: 87%) using best_scores
  from userProgress, updates on every renderCards() call
- #pbBanner inside score panel — shows "⭐ New Personal Best!" when
  is_new_pb is true, gold animated pill with scale-in keyframe
- showPBBlast() — 70-piece CSS confetti burst + large gold text overlay,
  auto-removes after 3.2s; fires alongside the score panel banner
- Personal Bests section in My Progress modal: scenes sorted by best
  score descending, shows ↑ arrow when latest attempt beats previous one

commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

commit.message:
fix: correct Finviz screener params and frontend proxy config

- Fix FINVIZ_SCREENER_VERSION from 2 to 111 (overview table format)
- Fix FINVIZ_SCREENER_FILTER_TYPE from "all" to "4" (valid numeric type)
  These wrong values caused Finviz to return HTML error pages, silently
  parsed as 0 symbols by the CSV reader (0 tracked → no signals)
- Add HTML response detection in finviz_service.py to log errors instead
  of silently returning empty results
- Fix frontend .env.example: leave VITE_API_URL empty in dev so Vite
  proxy handles /api forwarding (avoids CORS cross-origin issues)
- Fix start-embodier.ps1: wrap pip show in try/catch to prevent
  $ErrorActionPreference="Stop" from killing script on stderr warnings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: importación masiva vendedores - batch processing, normalización CUIT, feedback detallado

- API: reemplaza queries secuenciales (1 por fila) con batch processing
  (1 findMany + 1 updateMany por vendedor) para soportar 1000+ filas
- Normalización CUIT: busca tanto formato sin guiones como XX-XXXXXXXX-X
- Feedback detallado: asignados, ya asignados (sin cambios), CUITs no
  encontrados, CUITs inválidos, emails de vendedor no encontrados
- Elimina creación automática de clientes por CUIT no encontrado
- maxDuration=60s para evitar timeout en serverless

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SwiftData crash: re-fetch project after relationship deletions

validateFilesystem and cleanupDuplicateSessions delete sessions/groups,
leaving the project's inverse relationships pointing at faulted objects.
When discoverProjectGroups then sets group.project = project, SwiftData
tries to traverse project.taskGroups to maintain the inverse, hitting
deleted faults and causing SIGABRT. Re-fetching the project gives clean
relationship state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(api): fix pre-existing API test failures (#459)

* fix: accept 'dev' as valid development environment in config validators

The secret_key validator in api/app/config.py and the required secrets
validator in worker/worker/config.py only accepted "development" as a
dev environment string, but ECS sets ENVIRONMENT=dev. This caused the
API to crash-loop on startup after the security audit commit (1ce9bcd)
was deployed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(dashboard): merge duplicate user records on login to restore missing ideas

When users were created via the internal API with synthetic cognitoIds
(internal-xxx) and then logged in via Cognito with a real cognitoId,
two separate user records could exist for the same email. Ideas created
under the old internal user weren't visible after login.

getCurrentUser() now checks for and merges any internal-* duplicate
user on every login. Also adds /api/internal/debug and
/api/internal/merge-users endpoints for diagnostics and manual fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(dashboard): add Team model for shared idea visibility across users

All users on the same team now see all ideas, packs, and market scans.
Adds Team model, migrates existing data to a default team, and switches
all access checks from userId to teamId.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): fix pre-existing test failures in API test suite

- test_reach: use pytest.approx for floating point geo multiplier comparisons
- test_routes_discovery: add dev auth headers via conftest fixture
- test_routes_scoring: add dev auth headers to AsyncClient instances
- conftest: set APP_ENV=development and provide auth_headers fixture

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(kiosk): fix Snap Chromium session-restore dialog breaking kiosk mode

Root cause: kiosk script was clearing crash flags at the wrong path.
Snap Chromium stores prefs at ~/snap/chromium/current/.config/...
not ~/.config/chromium/... so the crash state was never cleared,
causing the "Restore previous session?" dialog to appear and break
fullscreen kiosk mode after every reboot.

Fixes:
- Check BOTH Snap and regular Chromium preference paths
- Add --user-data-dir=/tmp/archimedes-kiosk-profile to eliminate
  session-restore dialogs entirely (fresh profile each boot)
- Add --disable-restore-session-state flag as extra guard

New file: scripts/fix-kiosk.sh — immediate one-command repair script
that updates the kiosk launcher and restarts Chromium in kiosk mode
without requiring a full bootstrap re-run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix double escalation handoff in cancellation responses

feat: add complete macOS deployment script

Full macOS Production Deployment Support

Based on user request: I want to run the same system on macOS

New Features:
- Complete macOS deployment script (openclaw-deploy-macos.sh)
- Full production environment deployment (same as Linux)
- Nginx reverse proxy configuration
- Tailscale network setup
- launchd service management (macOS equivalent of systemd)
- Homebrew package management
- All security configurations

Technical Implementation:
1. Service Management: launchd with .plist files
2. Nginx Configuration: /usr/local/etc/nginx/servers/
3. Package Management: Homebrew
4. Logging: ~/Library/Logs/
5. macOS-Specific: SHARP_IGNORE_GLOBAL_LIBVIPS=1, Apple Silicon support

Supported Systems:
- macOS 10.15 (Catalina) or later
- Intel and Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3)

This is a complete port with full production features.

feat(scripts): harden rch execution scripts with timeout policy, local
fallback rejection, step logging, and remote exit code extraction

Major robustness overhaul of the shell-based CI/test runner scripts:

run_adversarial_campaign_gate.sh:
- Add configurable RCH_EXEC_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (default 900s) with timeout
  wrapper around all rch exec calls
- Add rch_strip_ansi() for reliable log parsing despite ANSI escape codes
- Add rch_remote_exit_code() to extract the remote process exit code from
  rch output logs, distinguishing rch wrapper failures from actual test
  failures
- Add rch_reject_local_fallback() to fail-closed when rch falls back to
  local execution (matching patterns like "falling back to local",
  "running locally", "RCH-E326", etc.)
- Add rch_recovered_success() to detect cases where remote execution
  succeeded but artifact retrieval timed out, avoiding false failures
- Add per-step log capture to step_logs/ directory with indexed filenames
- Add CARGO_BUILD_JOBS passthrough for parallel build control
- Add json_escape() helper for safe manifest JSON generation
- Record rch_exec_timeout_seconds in run manifest
- Include step_logs array and step_logs_dir in manifest artifacts

run_frx_frankenbrowser_integration_blueprint_suite.sh:
- Fix run_step calls to properly propagate failures with || return 1

run_frx_milestone_release_test_evidence_integrator_suite.sh:
- Add step logging infrastructure matching adversarial gate pattern

run_observability_information_theoretic_gate.sh:
- Add rch timeout and fallback rejection infrastructure

run_parser_event_ast_equivalence.sh:
- Add step log capture and rch hardening

run_parser_phase1_arena_suite.sh:
- Add comprehensive rch hardening with timeout policy drift detection
- Record rch_observed_timeout_seconds and rch_timeout_policy_drift fields
- Add FE-PARSER-PHASE1-ARENA-TIMEOUT-0001 failure code

run_runtime_diagnostics_suite.sh:
- Major expansion with full rch hardening, timeout policy drift detection,
  step logging, and enhanced manifest generation

Also updates .beads/issues.jsonl with issue tracker state changes.

commit.message:
Fix TOCTOU race in lock acquisition by switching to atomic mkdir

Replace file-based lock with a lockdir (.refresh.lockdir). mkdir is
atomic on macOS/Linux, eliminating the check-then-create race that
allowed two concurrent runs to both proceed.

Track AH: return_attn_weights — expose softmax probabilities

flash_attention gains return_attn_weights: bool = False.
When True, returns a 2-tuple (output, attn_weights) where:
  output:       [B, H, N, D] same dtype as q
  attn_weights: [B, H, N, S] float32 softmax probabilities

Dispatch: routes through new _sdpa_with_weights() helper (Python SDPA
that exposes the intermediate probs before dropout).  MFA kernel does
not expose attention probabilities; this is always a Python fallback.

4 new tests: returns_tuple, shape_and_dtype, sums_to_one,
output_matches_no_return. 209 tests pass.

commit.message:
Fix Vercel function routing: replace routes with rewrites

Using `routes` with `outputDirectory: "."` disabled Vercel's
function auto-detection, causing api/feedback.ts and api/axp-claude.ts
to be served as static MPEG-2 files (video/mp2t) instead of being
executed as serverless functions.

Replacing `routes` with `rewrites` fixes this — rewrites are evaluated
after functions, so /api/ endpoints are correctly auto-detected and
invoked.

Also updates path syntax from regex ($1) to Vercel rewrite syntax (:path*).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix duplicate bicubic row in results table

Add --bicubic_only flag to evaluate.py to skip model eval when computing
bicubic baseline. Use it in run_ablations.py bicubic step.
